pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 29: DAYS = 7; Calculated as 1008 data points (7 * 24 * 60 / 10)
setup_task.sh implements get_surplus() function (lines 67-87) that generates data algorithmically with solar sine wave (6 AM-6 PM, 4000W peak) and consumption patterns (base 300W + peaks)
task.json description states '100% efficiency' and export_result.sh simulate() function (lines 63-87) directly converts power to energy without loss factors
Calculated: (7 days * 24 hours * 3600 seconds) / 600 seconds = 1008 points

privileged_info_summary: Task uses synthetic energy data generated with realistic solar and consumption patterns. Data consists of 1008 points over 7 days at 10-minute intervals (600 seconds). Battery is 5.0 kWh with 100% efficiency, starting at 0% charge. Solar generation peaks at 4000W around noon (6 AM-6 PM sine wave) with random cloud cover (20% chance). Base consumption is 300W with morning peak (+1500W, 7-9 AM) and evening peak (+2000W, 6-10 PM). Simulation tracks total energy successfully discharged to grid (avoided import).
